title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 4, 2026
headline: Federal courts found the United States' core fast-track deportation architecture unlawful, triggering the benchmark's new ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE ruling — a −5.9 composite downgrade (23.4 → 17.5) with a Developing-to-Critical band crossing — as seven entities confirm at the absolute floor, 3M sits 0.3 points above Critical on two concurrent sovereign PFAS suits, and Oracle enters the final 11 days before the WARN Act June 15 deadline.
summary: One change proposal this cycle, one new primary methodology ruling, and seven floor confirmations. The United States downgrade is the cycle's sole scored movement: federal court rulings by Judge Brian Murphy (D. Mass.) and Judge Jia Cobb (D.D.C.) found core fast-track and third-country deportation procedures unlawful — Tier-5 judicial findings that supply the adjudicated element previously absent from U.S. immigration-enforcement assessments. Combined with the 17th ICE in-custody death of 2026 (approximately one every six days) with documented expansion into warehouse facilities despite the record death rate, two U.S. citizens fatally shot by federal immigration officers in Minneapolis with official victim-blaming, and National Guard deployments to five cities, the multi-dimension downgrade of −5.9 (ACC 1.9→1.4, EQU 1.7→1.4, EMP 1.9→1.5, INT 1.9→1.5, BND 2.3→2.0) clears the five-point proposal threshold and crosses from Developing into Critical. The new ruling — ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE — explicitly distinguishes this from three corporate entities assessed the same night whose legal exposure remains at the allegation stage: 3M (Australia $1.43B + Minnesota MPCA PFAS suits), UnitedHealth Group (DOJ MA-billing probe + MA AG $100M MassHealth suit + AZ AG eight-insurer price-fixing cartel suit), and CVS Health (340B $250M diversion suits). For 3M, a disciplined modeled downgrade of −2.3 would cross the same Developing-to-Critical boundary but falls below the five-point threshold; 3M sits 0.3 points above Critical with the clearest sub-threshold boundary case in the corporate cluster. Yemen joins the absolute floor cohort under a new ATTRIBUTION-NON-STATE-ACTOR-AT-FLOOR ruling: the UNSC's unanimous June 6 demand to release 73 detained UN staff targets Houthi-perpetrated detentions, which the existing active-perpetration and conflict-driven floor already encodes. The total floor cohort — Russia, Sudan, South Sudan, Afghanistan, Myanmar, xAI/Grok, Yemen — is the largest confirmed in a single cycle in the digest record. Humanitarian and policy risks are accelerating: the EU Returns Regulation enters force June 12, the Oracle WARN deadline falls June 15, the Afghanistan UNAMA mandate vote is June 17, and the Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC continues to escalate with Kampala spread and no approved vaccine.

topSignals.title: United States 23.4 → 17.5 (−5.9) — Band Crossing Proposed: Developing → Critical; ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE
topSignals.description: Federal courts have ruled the core architecture of the United States' fast-track deportation policy unlawful. Judge Brian Murphy (D. Mass., Feb 25, 2026) struck down third-country deportations conducted without notice or opportunity for recourse as a due-process violation. Judge Jia Cobb (D.D.C.) barred expedited removal of port-of-entry parolees. These are Tier-5 judicial findings of unlawful conduct — not allegations — and they supply the adjudicated element that distinguishes this cycle's U.S. assessment from the simultaneously-assessed corporate matters (3M, UnitedHealth, CVS) where liability remains unadjudicated. Alongside the judicial findings: the 17th ICE in-custody death of 2026 (approximately one every six days), with ACLU-documented expansion into warehouse facilities despite the record death rate — an adjudicated-tier correction-refusal signal; two U.S. citizens fatally shot by federal immigration officers in Minneapolis, with an official characterizing one victim as having 'weaponized her car' — deflection at the highest evidentiary tier; and National Guard deployments to five cities. A new methodology ruling — ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE — formalizes the principle that judicial findings of unlawful conduct are scorable where filed-but-unadjudicated allegations are not. The defensible multi-dimension downgrade (ACC 1.9→1.4, EQU 1.7→1.4, EMP 1.9→1.5, INT 1.9→1.5, BND 2.3→2.0; AWR/ACT/SYS held) reaches −5.9, clearing the five-point change-proposal threshold. Band crossing proposed: Developing → Critical, 23.4 → 17.5.

topSignals.title: 3M Company 20.3 — Two Concurrent Sovereign PFAS Suits; Boundary at 0.3 Points Above Critical; New Dual-Liability Framing
topSignals.description: Two sovereign PFAS enforcement actions filed within one month establish 3M as the clearest sub-threshold boundary case in the Fortune 500 cluster. Australia's federal government filed its largest-ever government environmental claim ($1.43B) against 3M, alleging that 28 military installations were contaminated with AFFF and that 3M withheld information about known health risks — an AB1 deflection allegation at the sovereign level. The Minnesota Pollution Control Agency simultaneously filed suit alleging that PFOS contamination at 310,000 parts per trillion (versus a 0.05 ppt standard) continues 'to this day' despite the 2018 $850M Minnesota settlement; 3M asserts military-contractor federal immunity and seeks federal removal. A new methodology framing — FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED plus ADJUDICATED-PRIOR-REPARATION — is established: the new harms are filed-but-unadjudicated civil complaints without merits adjudication, but 3M has adjudicated prior reparation ($850M Minnesota 2018; A$408M Australian community settlements) that functions as an AB5 reparative-action signal holding ACC above the absolute floor. A disciplined modeled downgrade of −2.3 (ACC 1.5→1.25, INT 1.5→1.25, EMP 2.0→1.75) would cross the same Developing-to-Critical boundary as the United States proposal, but falls below the five-point threshold. 3M sits 0.3 points above Critical — the narrowest active corporate margin. The decisive conversion triggers are adverse adjudication or settlement of either suit, or rejection of the military-contractor immunity defense.

topSignals.title: Yemen 0.0 — UNSC June 6 Demand to Release 73 UN Staff; ATTRIBUTION-NON-STATE-ACTOR-AT-FLOOR Ruling; Floor Cohort Expands
topSignals.description: The United Nations Security Council issued a unanimous demand on June 6 calling for the immediate release of 73 UN staff detained in Yemen. The detentions were carried out by the Houthi (Ansar Allah) movement — a non-state armed group controlling territory in northern Yemen — not by the internationally-recognized Yemeni government. A new methodology ruling — ATTRIBUTION-NON-STATE-ACTOR-AT-FLOOR — is established: where harm is perpetrated by an armed non-state actor controlling territory within a failed-state entity, that in-territory armed-actor conduct is already encoded by the existing active-perpetration and conflict-driven floor. The country entity aggregates all in-territory actors at the floor. Yemen is added to the 0.0 floor cohort. The absolute floor (harm-flag override, all dimensions 1.0) cannot move lower; the ruling reinforces rather than changes the designation. More than 22.3 million people in Yemen require humanitarian assistance. First improvement signals would be verified detainee release and confirmed humanitarian-corridor opening.

topSignals.title: Oracle Corporation 20.6 — WARN Act June 15 Deadline 11 Days Out; Narrowest Active Corporate Boundary
topSignals.description: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window for approximately 30,000 affected workers closes June 15 — 11 days from tonight's cycle. The June 15 date materializes an already-priced layoff; the scored adjustment, if warranted, would arise from adjudicated findings on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory (classifying remote workers to avoid WARN notification thresholds) rather than from the deadline itself. The Washington state class action and New Jersey state WARN 90-day proceedings are the two active adjudication channels for the avoidance theory. Oracle sits 0.6 points above Critical — the narrowest active corporate margin in the Fortune 500 cluster after 3M's 0.3-point boundary. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the avoidance theory before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger.

topSignals.title: UnitedHealth Group 10.2 — AZ AG Eight-Insurer Price-Fixing Cartel Suit; Three Converging Sovereign Actions; FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED Sustained
topSignals.description: The Arizona Attorney General filed suit on June 1 against eight insurers including UnitedHealth Group and data analytics firm MultiPlan, alleging algorithmic coordination to suppress provider reimbursement rates in a price-fixing cartel structure. The suit joins the existing DOJ MA-billing criminal probe and the Massachusetts AG $100M MassHealth fraud suit (alleging a decade-long 2015–2025 SCO upcoding scheme with treble damages reaching approximately $300M) — three simultaneous sovereign enforcement actions corroborating the same billing-and-pricing conduct pattern. The FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED ruling is sustained: high sovereign-enforcement density reinforces a near-floor ACC 1.125 / EMP 1.25 profile already priced at 10.2. Even an aggressive modeled downgrade reaches only −2.0, sub-threshold. Confirm-with-watch; the leading corporate scoring trigger is merits adjudication or settlement of any of the three actions.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 — Scale-Floor Confirmed; June 1–2 Execution of Uprising Protesters; 37 Political Prisoners Executed Since March
topSignals.description: Iran executed Mohsen Maleki and Mohammad Mohammadinia on June 1 and Mehdi Avari on June 2 — each convicted in connection with the 2022 uprisings. Approximately 37 political prisoners have been executed since March 19, approximately one every 49 hours. Naghdi and the Amiri brothers face imminent execution risk. The SCALE-FLOOR ruling holds at 2.5: June's execution surge deepens a single dominant extremity already priced in the composite and does not satisfy the multi-category threshold required for movement to the absolute 0.0 floor. The execution pattern reinforces the near-floor designation without generating a new proposal.

topSignals.title: Democratic Republic of Congo 2.3 — Ebola Bundibugyo 381 Cases / 64 Deaths; PHEIC; Kampala Transmission; NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY Sustained
topSignals.description: The Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in DRC has reached 381 cases and 64 deaths. WHO has declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. Confirmed transmission has reached Kampala, Uganda (population 3 million+), with 15 Uganda cases documented. No approved vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain. Contact tracing covers approximately 45% of known transmission chains. A $518M international response plan is active but post-USAID funding constraints limit surge capacity at the outbreak's peak window. DRC confirms at 2.3 under the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT ruling: the outbreak is an exogenous shock revealing priced state-response incapacity, absent documented deliberate obstruction. Documented deliberate state interference with the WHO response — access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression — would break the overlay hold from the near-floor 2.3 composite.

topSignals.title: xAI/Grok 0.0 — Floor Confirmed; Pentagon $200M Classified-Systems Access; National Security Contract Compounds Floored Profile
topSignals.description: xAI/Grok's Pentagon classified-systems access agreement ($200M) is assessed under the MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT ruling: for xAI/Grok, classified military-platform access compounds an already-floored governance and safety profile. This differs from AI labs where civilian-oversight safeguards partially offset defense contracts. Senator Warren's active safety oversight inquiry and documented harmful outputs across multiple categories further reinforce the floor designation. The entity is published at 0.0 (harm-flag override, all dimensions 1.0); no downward movement is possible. Floor confirmed.

boundaryWatch.trigger: The Murphy and Cobb deportation injunctions are subject to appellate review; a further ICE in-custody death reinforces the proposed 17.5 baseline. A successful stay of both injunctions combined with a documented deceleration in custody deaths would be the first reassessment signal in the upward direction.
boundaryWatch.note: United States sits 3.4 points below the Developing/Critical boundary at the proposed 17.5 composite. The band crossing rests on Tier-5 adjudicated judicial findings (Murphy Feb 25; Cobb D.D.C.) that the core deportation architecture is unlawful — a conduct pattern the ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE ruling formalizes this cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Adverse adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit, or rejection of 3M's military-contractor federal immunity defense, converts to a scorable ACC/INT/EMP downgrade into Critical.
boundaryWatch.note: 3M sits 0.3 points above the Critical boundary — the narrowest active margin in the Fortune 500 cluster. Two concurrent sovereign suits filed within one month (Australia government $1.43B; Minnesota MPCA PFOS at 310,000 ppt vs 0.05 ppt standard) raise enforcement density without yet supplying an adjudicated finding. Prior settled reparation ($850M Minnesota 2018; A$408M Australian community) holds ACC above the floor under the ADJUDICATED-PRIOR-REPARATION framing.

boundaryWatch.trigger: WARN Act last-working-day window closes June 15. NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings and Washington class action are the active adjudication channels on the remote-reclassification avoidance theory.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 20.6 is 0.6 points above Critical — the second-narrowest active corporate margin. The June 15 WARN deadline is the most proximate known hard trigger date across all assessed entities. Adverse WARN-avoidance adjudication would likely cross into Critical from the current baseline.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate state obstruction of humanitarian access, a state-attributable mass-casualty event, or confirmation of famine areas in Nigeria convert the PRICED-CONTINUATION carry-forward to a scorable downgrade.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 21.9 is 1.9 points above Critical. Approximately 34.7 million food-insecure in the lean season and 3.5 million IDPs reflect non-state armed group (Boko Haram/ISWAP) and structural-fragility drivers already priced at the 21.9 baseline. The PRICED-CONTINUATION ruling holds because dominant harm is non-state-driven, absent documented deliberate state obstruction of aid.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Verified delivery of the UK CMA publisher opt-out and attribution remedy (approximately nine months) plus replication in EU or another major jurisdiction constitutes the upgrade trigger from the 40.0/41 boundary. Adverse AI-Overviews antitrust finding in a parallel proceeding produces downward pressure.
boundaryWatch.note: Alphabet/Google remains at the exact 40.0/41 Developing-Functional boundary, sustained from the June 3 cycle. The COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling established June 3 holds: the UK CMA remedy is net-neutral this cycle; the upgrade condition is operationalized delivery, not announced intention. EU AI Act GPAI enforcement takes full effect August 2.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth — a favorable ruling credits the DoD-terms refusal as an I1 governance signal and triggers the Functional/Established crossing from 59.1.
boundaryWatch.note: Cycle 16. DC Circuit oral arguments remain divided with no ruling issued. Anthropic's holdout from the DoW classified-network access framework continues as a standing governance distinction from peer AI labs.

emergingRisks.risk: The United States' multi-dimension downgrade proposal — grounded in judicial findings of unlawful deportation conduct and the 17th ICE in-custody death of 2026 — documents a conduct pattern the ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE ruling now formalizes as a methodology category.
emergingRisks.detail: Federal courts ruled the core fast-track and third-country deportation architecture unlawful (Murphy D. Mass.; Cobb D.D.C.). The 17th in-custody death of 2026 comes with documented expansion of detention capacity despite the record death rate. Two U.S. citizens were fatally shot by federal officers with official deflection. National Guard deployments reached five cities. The proposed composite of 17.5 (Critical) is the authoritative lower-confidence reading pending appellate review of the Murphy and Cobb injunctions.

emergingRisks.risk: 3M sits 0.3 points above Critical with two concurrent sovereign PFAS suits — Australia $1.43B and Minnesota MPCA — and the military-contractor immunity defense as the primary barrier to conversion to a scored downgrade.
emergingRisks.detail: Australia's largest-ever government environmental claim alleges 28 contaminated military bases and risk-information withholding. The Minnesota MPCA suit documents PFOS at 310,000 parts per trillion versus a 0.05 ppt standard, with discharge continuing 'to this day' despite the 2018 settlement. 3M asserts military-contractor federal immunity and seeks federal removal of the Minnesota suit. Rejection of that immunity defense is the primary conversion trigger for the sub-threshold −2.3 modeled downgrade.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle sits 0.6 points above Critical with the WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window closing June 15 — the most proximate hard trigger date across all assessed entities.
emergingRisks.detail: Approximately 30,000 workers are in the WARN window. The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is pre-positioned for promotion to a scored category upon adjudication. NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings and the Washington class action are both active. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: The EU Returns Regulation enters force June 12, creating structured enforcement mechanisms for third-country return procedures that will be consequential for entities with standing non-refoulement profiles.
emergingRisks.detail: The Regulation imposes procedural and substantive constraints on EU-member and EU-adjacent return procedures. India (15.6 applied), Romania (37.5 rotation), and Germany (77.1 post-reconciliation) each carry standing non-refoulement or minority-return profiles. Any documented EU-facilitated return to a country where a third-country national faces persecution without individual assessment converts from watch to scored signal under the RETURN-HUBS-AND-NON-REFOULEMENT anchor.

emergingRisks.risk: The Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC is entering its peak transmission window with confirmed Kampala urban spread, no approved vaccine for the Bundibugyo strain, and 45% contact tracing coverage in a post-USAID funding environment.
emergingRisks.detail: DRC at 381 cases/64 deaths; Uganda at 15 cases with Kampala (3M+ population) confirmed. The $518M international response plan is active but mobilization capacity is constrained by the post-USAID funding reduction. Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response in either DRC or Uganda would break the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at DRC's near-floor 2.3 composite.

emergingRisks.risk: Afghanistan's UNSC UNAMA mandate renewal vote on June 17 is the most significant near-term external accountability event for a floor-designated entity; Taliban's codified gender-persecution decrees sustain the absolute floor independent of the vote's outcome.
emergingRisks.detail: Decree No.12 criminalizes domestic violence only where injury is visible with a 15-day sentence; Decree No.18 imposes spousal-separation restrictions. Both are codified active perpetration. The June 17 UNSC vote is an external-process milestone; a non-renewal of the UNAMA mandate would reduce the accountability architecture without altering the entity's own conduct trajectory.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect August 2 — 59 days from tonight — with no assessed AI lab having published compliance documentation.
emergingRisks.detail: Non-compliance fines can reach €35M or 7% of global annual turnover. The August 2 date is the first hard regulatory enforcement date the AI labs cluster has faced. OpenAI's comprehensive full assessment is overdue. The compliance gap across the full cluster is structural and accumulating.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan and South Sudan sustain dual-country CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR designation as the West and Central Africa lean season builds toward its August peak, with IPC Phase 5 populations expanding.
emergingRisks.detail: Sudan: 33.7M requiring assistance; 19.5M acutely food insecure; Darfur famine areas confirmed; third year of SAF/RSF war. South Sudan: 7.8M (56%) acutely food insecure; 73,300 IPC Phase 5; four counties at famine risk; access denial sustained. Both at 0.0 floor. Further IPC Phase 5 area expansion is probable through the August lean-season peak at current funding levels.

sectorAlerts.sector: ICE Detention Deaths and Due Process — US Immigration Enforcement
sectorAlerts.headline: 17th ICE in-custody death of 2026 with documented detention expansion; two U.S. citizen fatalities; judicial findings of unlawful deportation conduct
observations: The 17th ICE in-custody death of 2026 — approximately one death every six days — is accompanied by documented expansion into warehouse facilities despite the record death rate, constituting an adjudicated-tier correction-refusal signal (AB2) in the scoring framework

Two U.S. citizens fatally shot by federal immigration officers in Minneapolis; official characterization of one victim as having 'weaponized her car' is the specific AB1/E4 deflection signal scored in the EMP and INT dimensions

The combination of judicial findings (Murphy, Cobb) + record-pace in-custody deaths + citizen fatalities + expansion-despite-deaths produces the first U.S. band crossing in the benchmark's countries index
ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE is now available as a standing methodology ruling for future entities where courts issue adverse findings of unlawful conduct

methodologyNotes.name: ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE
methodologyNotes.description: New ruling established this cycle for the United States. Where a court of competent jurisdiction issues a finding that an institutional actor's conduct is unlawful — rather than merely filing a complaint or opening an investigation — the judicial finding constitutes a Tier-5 evidentiary anchor that is scorable. The distinguishing principle: a filed-but-unadjudicated allegation is not scorable pre-adjudication; an adjudicated judicial finding that specific conduct was unlawful is scorable at the moment of that finding. United States (Judge Murphy D. Mass. Feb 25, 2026 striking down third-country deportations without notice/recourse as a due-process violation; Judge Cobb D.D.C. barring expedited removal of port-of-entry parolees) is the anchor case. 3M, UnitedHealth Group, and CVS Health — assessed the same cycle — are the counter-examples: their legal exposure remains at the civil-complaint stage without merits adjudication, and they are confirmed rather than downgraded. The ruling is available for future application to any entity where a court issues an adverse finding of unlawful conduct.

methodologyNotes.name: FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED plus ADJUDICATED-PRIOR-REPARATION
methodologyNotes.description: New framing established this cycle for 3M Company. Where an entity has (a) new harm allegations that are filed-but-unadjudicated (not yet scorable) and (b) adjudicated or settled prior reparation in the same harm category, the prior reparation functions as an AB5 reparative-action signal that holds the relevant dimension above the absolute floor. 3M has two new sovereign PFAS suits (Australia $1.43B; Minnesota MPCA) that are newly-filed civil complaints without merits adjudication. 3M also has adjudicated/settled prior reparation: $850M Minnesota settlement 2018 and A$408M Australian community settlements. The prior reparation holds ACC above the 1.0 absolute floor. A disciplined modeled downgrade of −2.3 is defensible but sub-threshold. Distinguished from the United States case by the absence of an adjudicated finding on the new harm. 3M is the anchor case.

methodologyNotes.name: ATTRIBUTION-NON-STATE-ACTOR-AT-FLOOR
methodologyNotes.description: New ruling established this cycle for Yemen. Where harm in a country entity is perpetrated by a non-state armed group controlling territory within a failed-state or conflict-state entity, the in-territory armed-actor harm is encoded by the existing active-perpetration and conflict-driven floor. The country entity aggregates all in-territory actors at the floor. Yemen (Houthi-perpetrated detentions of 73 UN staff; UNSC unanimous demand June 6) is the anchor case. The Houthi movement controls territory in northern Yemen and is a warring party; the detentions are in-territory armed-actor conduct already encoded by the active-perpetration floor. Yemen is added to the 0.0 floor cohort. First improvement signal: verified detainee release plus confirmed humanitarian-corridor opening.

methodologyNotes.name: PRICED-CONTINUATION / NON-STATE-DRIVEN
methodologyNotes.description: New ruling established this cycle for Nigeria. Where food insecurity, displacement, and armed-group violence are the dominant harm drivers, but the dominant cause is non-state armed groups (Boko Haram/ISWAP, banditry) against a low-capacity state — not documented deliberate state obstruction of humanitarian access — the June 2026 figures reinforce rather than reveal new state-attributable mass harm. Nigeria (approximately 34.7M lean-season food-insecure; approximately 3.5M IDPs) is the anchor case. The PRICED-CONTINUATION/NON-STATE-DRIVEN ruling is distinguished from the Sudan/South Sudan CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR by the absence of documented deliberate state obstruction of aid. Converts on state-attributable mass harm, famine-area confirmation, or documented deliberate obstruction.

methodologyNotes.name: ACTIVE-PERPETRATION-FLOOR
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for Russia, Myanmar, Afghanistan, and xAI/Grok. Where an entity is published at the absolute 0.0 floor (harm-flag override, all dimensions 1.0) and new adverse evidence documents further conduct in the same categories, the evidence reinforces the floor designation but cannot produce a downward proposal. Russia (continued active perpetration of the war on Ukraine), Myanmar (June 1 airstrikes on IDP camps; 5.2M displaced; ICC arrest-warrant application is external process not a conduct change), Afghanistan (codified gender persecution via Decrees 12 and 18; UNAMA June 17 is external milestone), and xAI/Grok (Pentagon $200M classified access; documented harmful outputs) are the reinforcing cases this cycle.

methodologyNotes.name: CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for Sudan and South Sudan. Sudan (33.7M requiring assistance; 19.5M acutely food insecure; Darfur famine areas confirmed; SAF/RSF third year) and South Sudan (7.8M/56% acutely food insecure; 73,300 IPC Phase 5; four counties at famine risk; SAF/SPLM-IO access denial) sustain the active-perpetration 0.0 floor via state-attributable constrained humanitarian access. Both entities are at the absolute floor; escalation reinforces.

methodologyNotes.name: NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for DRC. The Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak (381 cases/64 deaths; PHEIC; Kampala spread; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; 45% contact tracing) plus approximately 26.5M concurrent food insecure are an exogenous shock revealing already-priced state-response incapacity, absent documented deliberate state obstruction. The overlay holds at 2.3. Documented deliberate state interference with the WHO response — access denial, fund diversion, data suppression — breaks the hold and constitutes a scored downgrade trigger from the near-floor 2.3 composite.

methodologyNotes.name: SCALE-FLOOR
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for Iran at 2.5. Where a country entity has a single dominant extremity (systematic political executions) that deepens but does not expand into additional harm categories, the entity remains at the scale-floor anchor rather than crossing to the absolute 0.0 floor. The multi-category threshold for 0.0 designation is not met by escalation within a single priced extremity. Iran (June 1–2 execution of uprising-linked prisoners; approximately 37 political prisoners executed since March 19; imminent risk to Naghdi and Amiri brothers) deepens the already-priced BND extremity without meeting the multi-category 0.0 threshold.

forwardTriggers.entity: EU Returns Regulation (India, Romania, Germany context)
forwardTriggers.trigger: EU Returns Regulation enters force; any documented EU-facilitated return to a country where a third-country national faces persecution without individual assessment constitutes a scored signal under the RETURN-HUBS-AND-NON-REFOULEMENT anchor.

WARN Act last-working-day window closes for approximately 30,000 affected workers; any adjudicated finding on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory in NJ state proceedings or the Washington class action constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline.
UNSC UNAMA mandate renewal vote; non-renewal reduces accountability architecture; reversal of Decree No.12 or Decree No.18 constitutes the first scored improvement signal from the absolute floor.

Ebola Bundibugyo peak window through July with confirmed Kampala transmission; documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response breaks the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at the near-floor 2.3 composite.

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect; fines up to €35M or 7% of global annual turnover; no assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation.
West and Central Africa lean season peak; Darfur famine areas confirmed; Sudan humanitarian plan approximately 20% funded; further IPC Phase 5 area expansion probable through August.

Appellate review of Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions; further ICE in-custody deaths reinforce the proposed 17.5 Critical baseline; a successful stay of both injunctions combined with documented deceleration in custody deaths would be the first reassessment signal in the upward direction.

Adverse adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit, or rejection of 3M's military-contractor federal immunity defense, converts the sub-threshold −2.3 modeled downgrade to a scored change proposal into Critical from the 0.3-point-above-Critical baseline.

Merits adjudication or settlement of any of the three converging sovereign enforcement actions — DOJ MA-billing criminal probe, MA AG $100M MassHealth fraud suit, AZ AG eight-insurer price-fixing cartel suit — constitutes the leading corporate scoring trigger of the current cycle.

Verified delivery of the UK CMA publisher opt-out and attribution remedy (approximately nine months) plus replication in EU or another major jurisdiction constitutes the upgrade trigger from the 40.0/41 Developing-Functional boundary.
DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth (Pentagon supply-chain-risk designation) — favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement.
Comprehensive full assessment overdue (51+ days); DoW classified-network agreement public terms; PBC conversion finalization; EU AI Act August 2 enforcement accumulating.

Verified release of all detained UN staff and confirmed humanitarian-corridor opening constitute the first scored improvement signals from the absolute floor; further Houthi detentions or famine expansion reinforce.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: Where federal courts find a deportation policy unlawful under the due-process clause — as Judge Murphy and Judge Cobb did on third-country removals and parolee expedited removal — does the adjudicated finding flow to the ACC and EQU dimensions at the same magnitude regardless of whether the policy was subsequently stayed, or does a judicial stay of the injunction alter the scored weight of the original finding?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: The ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE ruling formalizes the scoring trigger; whether an appellate stay of the injunction modifies the weight of the underlying finding is the methodology question now open.
